UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile within the NEU Lab Log system, storing essential information and access control data. This entity links to Firebase Authentication via the 'id' field.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, typically the Firebase Authentication User ID (UID).

name.description: The full name of the user as provided by their Google account.

email.description: The institutional email address of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the system, which can be 'Professor' or 'Admin'.

isBlocked.description: Indicates whether the user's access to the system is currently blocked by an administrator. True means blocked, False means active.

qrString.description: A unique string associated with the user, used for QR code based validation and access to rooms.

RoomLog.title: RoomLog
RoomLog.description: Records an instance of a laboratory room usage event, detailing who used it, which room, and when.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RoomLog entity.

professorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the professor who initiated the room usage log. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N RoomLog)

roomNumber.description: The specific identifier or name of the laboratory room being used.

timestamp.description: The exact date and time when the room usage was logged.

status.description: The current status of the room usage log (e.g., 'Active'). This allows for potential future statuses like 'Completed' or 'Cancelled'.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles, including their role, blocked status, and a unique QR string. The `id` field matches the Firebase Authentication UID. Includes denormalized 'role' and 'isBlocked' fields for authorization independence. The 'qrString' is primarily for internal validation or secure Cloud Function-based login.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) corresponding to the user.

definition.description: Records instances of laboratory room usage. Each document includes `professorId` to link to the initiating user, `roomNumber`, `timestamp`, and `status`. The `professorId` is denormalized for authorization independence and efficient rule checks.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific room usage log entry.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The mere existence of a document at this path (`/roles_admin/{uid}`) signifies that the corresponding user is an 'Admin'. Used by security rules for efficient role checks (e.g., `get(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)).exists()`). Documents typically contain minimal or no data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. User profiles are stored in `/user_profiles/{userId}`, room usage logs in `/room_logs/{logId}`, and administrative roles in `/roles_admin/{userId}` for efficient DBAC (Database Access Control).

**Authorization Independence:**
*   Each `UserProfile` document at `/user_profiles/{userId}` is self-contained with authorization-relevant fields such as `id`, `role`, and `isBlocked`. Security rules can directly check `request.auth.uid` against `userId` for ownership, or query the `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection for administrator status using an `exists()` call. This avoids hierarchical `get()` calls to parent documents within security rules, enabling atomic operations and simplifying rule logic.
*   `RoomLog` documents at `/room_logs/{logId}` denormalize the `professorId` within each log entry. This allows security rules to validate the creator (`request.auth.uid == request.resource.data.professorId`) or check for admin access (`get(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)).exists()`) without needing to fetch the associated `UserProfile` document for *authorization* purposes (though a `get()` to validate the *creating user's* role/blocked status is a single, isolated check on their *own* profile).

**QAPs (Secure Queryability for List Operations):**
*   **`/user_profiles`:** This collection supports secure `list` operations for administrators. Rules will allow `list` if `request.auth.uid` corresponds to an admin (checked via `get(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)).exists()`), enabling the Admin Usage Dashboard to view and manage all users. Regular users can only `get` their own profile. For the QR code login feature: directly querying for arbitrary `qrString` values from the client is a QAP anti-pattern as it risks exposing sensitive information or enabling enumeration. Therefore, it is recommended that the actual "QR login" validation (where a user logs in by scanning a QR code *instead of manual sign-in*) be handled via a secure **Cloud Function**. The client app sends the scanned `qrString` to the Cloud Function, which then securely performs the Firestore lookup and `isBlocked` check, and if valid, issues a custom Firebase Auth token. This keeps the `qrString` private and adheres strictly to QAPs. If a user is already logged in via Google and uses a QR code for *in-app validation* (e.g., confirming their identity for a room entry), they would securely retrieve *their own* `qrString` from their `/user_profiles/{request.auth.uid}` document.
*   **`/room_logs`:** This collection is designed for secure `list` operations for administrators. Security rules will strictly enforce that only users with an entry in `/roles_admin` can `list` or `read` room logs. This enables the Admin Usage Dashboard to display, filter, and monitor all laboratory usage data without exposing it to unauthorized users. Professors can only `create` logs, not list others'.
*   **`/roles_admin`:** This collection is used purely for `exists()` checks within security rules. Client-side `list` or `get` operations on this collection are explicitly disallowed, making it a highly secure mechanism for role-based access control.
